name: Wing Language
pricing_notes: Open source; Winglang Cloud (Wing Platform) available for managed deployment

auth.notes: Wing handles cloud auth transparently; AWS/GCP/Azure credentials via standard SDKs

performance.notes: Compile-time framework; runtime performance depends on compiled cloud resources

agent_readiness.notes: Cloud-oriented programming language. Compiles to Terraform/CloudFormation + app code. `cloud.Function`, `cloud.Bucket`, etc. abstractions. Local simulator for testing without cloud. TypeScript-compatible. Unique 'preflight/inflight' distinction for compile-time vs runtime.

gotchas: Very early stage (0.x) — language still evolving significantly
New programming language requires learning investment
Tooling maturity lags established IaC tools
VSCode extension provides limited IDE support vs TypeScript
best_when: Greenfield cloud-native apps where IaC/app code unification is valuable
Agents that provision and interact with cloud resources
Teams tired of context-switching between Terraform and application code
avoid_when: Production systems requiring mature tooling and community
Existing teams with established Pulumi/Terraform workflows
Teams unable to invest in learning a new language
summary: Wing is a cloud-oriented programming language that unifies infrastructure and application code. Compiles to Terraform/CloudFormation + executable code from single source. Unique preflight/inflight distinction cleanly separates cloud resource provisioning from runtime logic. Local simulator enables testing without cloud credentials. Innovative concept but very early stage with small ecosystem.
